message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  We introduce a lightweight model based on Mask R-CNN with ResNet18 and ResNet34 backbone models that segments lesions and predicts COVID-19 from chest CT scans in a single shot. The model requires a small dataset to train: 650 images for the segmentation branch and 3000 for the classification branch, and it is evaluated on 21292 images to achieve a
                  <jats:bold>42</jats:bold>
                  .
                  <jats:bold>45%</jats:bold>
                  average precision (main MS COCO criterion) on the segmentation test split (100 images),
                  <jats:bold>93</jats:bold>
                  .
                  <jats:bold>00%</jats:bold>
                  COVID-19 sensitivity and F1-score of
                  <jats:bold>96</jats:bold>
                  .
                  <jats:bold>76%</jats:bold>
                  on the classification test split (21192 images) across 3 classes: COVID-19, Common Pneumonia and Control/Negative. The full source code, models and pretrained weights are available on
                  <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://github.com/AlexTS1980/COVID-Single-Shot-Model">https://github.com/AlexTS1980/COVID-Single-Shot-Model</jats:ext-link>
                  .
                </jats:p>
title: Single-Shot Lightweight Model For The Detection of Lesions And The Prediction of COVID-19 From Chest CT Scans
